other_observers: none; I found and identified the bird.
specific_location: in the banding station about 0.3 mi north of the Visitor Center at Barr Lake State Park along the east side of the lake
description: I caught this bird at the Barr Lake banding station. It was a large, brown thrush, with distinctly rufous upperparts, particularly the crown and nape, a white eye ring, distinctive black-and-white streaked auriculars, white underparts with bold, distinct, black spotting on the chest and sides. The belly was white. The tail was shorter than the wing chord, the legs were pink, and the eyes were dark.  I determined the ossification of the skull, which was complete, so the bird was a full adult (at least 1.25 yrs of age).Behaviors: caught in mist netCall: none heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
